name: Sen. Rick Scott

name: Sen. Ashley Moody

name: Rep. Mike Rogers

name: Rep. Steve Scalise
title: House Majority Leader (LA-01)
verdict: BIG OIL & THE FOSSIL FUEL MONOPOLY
notes: One of the highest career recipients of fossil fuel money in congressional history.

name: Rep. Tim Walberg
title: Representative (MI-05) / Chair, Education
topDonors: Apollo Global Management, DeVos Family, Club for Growth
notes: Architect of federal school privatization. Heavily backed by billionaire networks intent on diverting public tax dollars into unregulated private and charter schools.

name: Rep. Bobby Scott
title: Representative (VA-03) / Ranking, Education
topDonors: Teachers Unions, Labor Organizations, Education Professionals
notes: Primary firewall against public school privatization. Backing is almost exclusively derived from organized labor defending traditional public education infrastructure.

name: Chuck Schumer
title: Senate Minority Leader
notes: Current Senate Minority Leader. Anchored to New York voters. Decades of protecting private equity loopholes and shielding the financial sector from structural reform.

name: Hakeem Jeffries
title: House Minority Leader
topDonors: Pro-Israel America PAC, American Israel Public Affairs Committee, Apollo Global Management
notes: Current House Minority Leader. Anchored to New York voters. Consistently aligns with corporate PACs to suppress progressive economic challenges to the current power structure.

name: John Thune
title: Senate Majority Leader
topDonors: Blue Cross/Blue Shield, Nextera Energy, Comcast
notes: Current Senate Majority Leader. Anchored to South Dakota voters. Consistent voting record enabling corporate monopolies in telecommunications and blocking universal healthcare expansion.

name: Mike Johnson
title: Speaker of the House
topDonors: Club for Growth, Koch PAC, Oil & Gas Industry
notes: Current Speaker of the House. Anchored to Louisiana voters. Uses leadership position to advance aggressive corporate tax cuts and shield fossil fuel and private equity from oversight.
